subject: Re: BoxNet picking issues with UltraAuFoil grids
body:
Hi Dimitry,

I did manually re select the movies, but they were continually de selected. 
I also made sure to remove any previous WARP files before starting. Here is 
the output on the command line window:

Running on GPU #1 (0 MB free) through 
WarpWorker12901999-5399-414c-8704-16e92a712451

Received "SetHeaderlessParams", with 3 arguments, for GPU #1, 0 MB free
Set headerless parameters to 7676, 7420, 0, int8
0.001

Received "LoadGainRef", with 5 arguments, for GPU #1, 0 MB free
Loaded gain reference and defect map: 5760, 4092, 1, normal, real, 1 A/px, 
ID = -1, False, True, False,
0.516

Received "LoadStack", with 3 arguments, for GPU #1, 0 MB free
Loaded stack: 5760, 4092, 35, normal, real, 1 A/px, ID = -1, 1
4.437

Received "MovieProcessMovement", with 2 arguments, for GPU #1, 0 MB free
System.Runtime.InteropServices.SEHException (0x80004005): External 
component has thrown an exception.
   at Warp.GPU.CreateShift(IntPtr d_frame, int2 dimsframe, Int32 nframes, 
int3[] h_origins, Int32 norigins, int2 dimsregion, Int64[] h_mask, UInt32 
masklength, IntPtr d_outputall, IntPtr d_sigma)
   at Warp.Movie.ProcessShift(Image originalStack, 
ProcessingOptionsMovieMovement options) in 
D:\Dev\warp5\WarpLib\Movie.cs:line 2171
   at WarpWorker.Program.Main(String[] args) in 
D:\Dev\warp5\WarpWorker\Program.cs:line 186
Received "LoadStack", with 3 arguments, for GPU #1, 0 MB free
Loaded stack: 5760, 4092, 35, normal, real, 1 A/px, ID = -1, 1
4.272

Best,
Matt
On Wednesday, September 28, 2022 at 10:46:29 AM UTC-4 teg...@gmail.com 
wrote:

> Hi Matt,
>
> Items are deselected when their processing leads to an error. Do you see 
> anything useful in the command line windows that pop up when processing 
> starts? Have you tried picking a single micrograph in the realspace tab 
> using the dedicated button there? You've put 1.1.0 in a new folder rather 
> than overwrite files in the 1.0.9 folder, right? I would also try updating 
> the GPU driver.
>
> Cheers,
> Dimitry
>
> On Tuesday, September 27, 2022 at 6:13:01 PM UTC-7 mjja...@gmail.com 
> wrote:
>
>> Hi Dimitry,
>>
>> Thanks for the response. I am attempting to use 1.1.0 beta, but I am 
>> having an issue where every movie is deselected and, therefore, won't 
>> process anything. With the same settings (but different boxnet model), 
>> 1.0.9 can process the movies. Also, after a few attempts in 1.1.0, the 
>> program cannot detect the movie path anymore (this was fixed with a 
>> restart).  Do you have any idea why all my movies are deselected in 1.1.0, 
>> but no 1.0.9?
>>
>> Thanks,
>> Matt
>>
>> On Saturday, September 24, 2022 at 12:08:17 PM UTC-4 teg...@gmail.com 
>> wrote:
>>
>>> Hi Both,
>>>
>>> Have you tried 1.1.0 already? It should do better in this case. The 
>>> contrast is off in the viewer because 1.0.9 estimates mean and standard 
>>> deviation of the whole micrograph, so the gold region throws it off. 1.1.0 
>>> uses a smaller central region, so should be less affected. CTF estimation 
>>> isn't involved in any of this.
>>>
>>> Cheers,
>>> Dimitry
>>>
>>> On Saturday, September 24, 2022 at 5:38:57 AM UTC-7 b.m.ha...@gmail.com 
>>> wrote:
>>>
>>>> I think that is best answered by Dimitry. We haven't had much luck 
>>>> playing with the CTF-estimation parameters for Ultraufoils and we don't get 
>>>> the same problems with grids where the foil is made from lighter metals and 
>>>> alloys.
>>>>
>>>> On Friday, September 23, 2022 at 4:43:45 PM UTC+3 mjja...@gmail.com 
>>>> wrote:
>>>>
>>>>> Thanks for the response Martin.
>>>>>
>>>>> Do you thing this is due to the BoxNet picker or the CTF estimation? 
>>>>> The contrast of micrographs with significant gold looks off, which suggests 
>>>>> to me that there is a CTF estimation issue.
>>>>>
>>>>> Thanks,
>>>>> Matt
>>>>>
>>>>> On Fri, Sep 23, 2022, 12:04 AM Martin Hallberg <b.m.ha...@gmail.com> 
>>>>> wrote:
>>>>>
>>>>>> Hi Matt,
>>>>>> This is common with Warp BoxNet particle picking. We try to avoid to 
>>>>>> have the gold in the image but if we anyway end up with it on a part of the 
>>>>>> data we pick using other tools. If needed for further processing in the 
>>>>>> Warp/M suite, one can always import particles (or just positions) to Warp 
>>>>>> usually then after some 2D or 3D "cleaning". I would love to hear if 
>>>>>> someone has a solution!
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Best,
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Martin
>>>>>>
>>>>>>
>>>>>>
>>>>>> On Thursday, September 22, 2022 at 8:00:39 PM UTC+3 mjja...@gmail.com 
>>>>>> wrote:
>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Hello,
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> I'm having BoxNet picking issues when there is a considerable amount 
>>>>>>> of the UltraAuFoil grid scaffold in the images:
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> BoxNet seems to be missing a lot of particles. This doesn’t happen 
>>>>>>> when less gold is in the image. Any way to fix this?
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Here are my CTF settings:
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Thank you,
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Matt
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> --

>>>>>> Groups "Warp" group.

>>>>>> <https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/2422e6ea-31f1-42be-9f9f-cb0f78da0593n%40googlegroups.com?utm_medium=email&utm_source=footer>
>>>>>> .
>>>>>>
>>>>>
